(uncountable, loosely) The substance of the atmosphere on a planet other than Earth.

examples.english: To live without air is impossible.
examples.chinese: 沒有空氣生活是不可能的。

current_entries.en_definition: A miniature, handheld paint sprayer, powered by compressed air or other gas, used for delicate, artistic painting, or sometimes retouching of photographs.
(anatomy) of or relating to the armpit; axillary.
Sudden surprise with fear or terror excited by apprehension of danger; in the military use, commonly, sudden apprehension of being attacked by surprise.

examples.english: The fire alarm sounded.
examples.chinese: 火警警报响了。

current_entries.en_definition: A mechanical device for awaking people, or rousing their attention.
A global, peer-led fellowship dedicated to an abstinence-based recovery from alcoholism.
A en given name, most famously held by Alexander the Great.
Another name; especially, an assumed name.
(legal) The plea or mode of defense under which a person on trial for a crime proves or attempts to prove being in another place when the alleged act was committed.

examples.english: The police are suspicious of my alibi.
examples.chinese: 警察覺得我的不在場證明可疑。

current_entries.en_definition: Not belonging to the same country, land, or government, or to the citizens or subjects thereof; foreign.
Very unfamiliar, strange, or removed.
Pertaining to extraterrestrial life; typical of an extraterrestrial creature.
A person, animal, plant, or other thing which is from outside the family, group, organization, or territory under consideration.
(sometimes, derogatory) A mischievous or suspicious foreigner.
Any life form of extraterrestrial or extradimensional origin.
Often followed by from or off: to get off an animal which one has been riding; to dismount; to descend or exit from a vehicle; hence, to complete one's journey; to stop.

examples.english: She sets your world alight.
examples.chinese: 她照亮你的世界。

current_entries.en_definition: Having resemblance or similitude; similar; without difference.
(poker) A hand where at least one player bets all of his or her chips.

Either of two species of large amphibious reptile, Alligator mississippiensis or Alligator sinensis, in the genus Alligator within order Crocodilia, which have sharp teeth and very strong jaws and are native to the Americas and China, respectively.
(engineering) A planned deviation between an exact dimension and a nominal or theoretical dimension.
A metal that is a combination of two or more elements, at least one of which is a metal, a base metal.
(transitive) To entice; to attract.
having to power to allure
At or from a distance, but within view, or at a small distance; apart; away.
Completely, wholly, or without exception.
Someone who is unqualified or insufficiently skillful.

examples.english: My uncle is an amateur cricket player.
examples.chinese: 我的舅舅爱好打板球。

current_entries.en_definition: (sometimes, attributive) A region including much of this river; specifically, the region of the Amazon Rainforest, or of the Amazon River Basin.

examples.english: The Amazon is the second longest river in the world after the Nile.
examples.chinese: 亞馬遜河的長度僅次於尼羅河，是世界第二長的河。

current_entries.en_definition: A hard, generally yellow to brown translucent or transparent fossil resin from extinct coniferous trees of the pine genus, used for jewellery, decoration and later dissolved as a binder in varnishes. One variety, blue amber, appears blue rather than yellow under direct sunlight. [from 15th c.]
An unhurried leisurely walk or stroll.
The form of the English language that is chiefly used in the United States, contrasted with British English or Canadian English and that of other places.
A sign language used primarily in the United States of America and Canada.
A transparent purple to violet variety of quartz with traces of manganese, used as a gemstone.
An act of the sovereign power granting oblivion, or a general pardon, for a past offense, as to subjects concerned in an insurrection.
(all article senses).

examples.english: I once wanted to be an astrophysicist.
examples.chinese: 我曾經想過當個太空物理學家。

current_entries.en_definition: (of words) A word or phrase that is created by rearranging the letters of another word or phrase.
(pharmacology) Any medicine, such as aspirin, that reduces pain, especially without inducing a loss of other sensation. (Contrast anesthetic.)
(of a device or system) In which the value of a data item (such as time) is represented by a continuous(ly) variable physical quantity that can be measured (such as the shadow of a sundial).
Any of the various forms of the Greek language of classical antiquity, particularly the classical Attic dialect used in Athenian literature.
Related to the Anglo-Saxon peoples or language.
Extreme pain, either of body or mind; excruciating distress.
(pharmaceutical drug) Any medicine or other agent that relieves pain.
One more further, in addition to the quantity by then; a second or additional one, similar in likeness or in effect.

examples.english: I told them to send me another ticket.
examples.chinese: 我請他們再寄給我一張票。

current_entries.en_definition: Not the same; different.
To make an investment in money, effort, or time before knowing one's chances.
Any thing that precedes another thing, especially the cause of the second thing.
(pharmaceutical drug) Any substance that can destroy or inhibit the growth of bacteria and similar microorganisms, generally transported by the lymphatic system.
(archaic) Grotesque, bizarre
A caricature.
A grotesque performer or clown, buffoon.
(pharmaceutical drug) A drug that inhibits the growth of fungi.
(pharmaceutical drug) A medicine that reduces fever; a febrifuge.
To impose a policy of segregation of groups of people, especially one based on race.
A call to a person or an authority for a decision, help, or proof; an entreaty, an invocation.
A trainee, especially in a skilled trade.

examples.english: He's your senior fellow apprentice.
examples.chinese: 他是你的师兄。

current_entries.en_definition: The fourth month of the Gregorian calendar, following March and preceding May.

examples.english: School begins on April the tenth.
examples.chinese: 學校在四月十日開學。

current_entries.en_definition: Relating to water; living in or near water, taking place in water.
Of, from, or pertaining to Arab countries or cultural behaviour (see also Arab as an adjective).

examples.english: Arabic is a very important language.
examples.chinese: 阿拉伯语是一种很重要的语言。

current_entries.en_definition: A major Semitic language originating from the Arabian peninsula, and now spoken natively (in various spoken dialects, all sharing a single highly conservative standardized literary form) throughout large sections of the Middle East and North Africa.
The Aramaic-derived script used to write the Arabic, Persian, Pashto, Urdu, and Uyghur languages, among others.
Referring to the Aramaic language, alphabet, culture or poetry.
The language of the Arameans from the tenth century BC: often called Old Aramaic.

Any member of a West Semitic semi-nomadic and pastoralist people who lived in the Levant and later also in upper Mesopotamia during the Late Bronze Age and the Iron Age. Their homeland is referred to in the Hebrew Bible as Aram. They spoke Aramaic.
Pertaining to the Arawak peoples
(philosophy) The philosophical system of Aristotle (384 BCE – 322 BCE) and his followers.
A city in Bouches-du-Rhône department, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, France.
(anatomy) The portion of the upper human appendage, from the shoulder to the wrist and sometimes including the hand.

examples.english: I have a very sore arm where you hit me.
examples.chinese: 你打在我手臂上的地方超級痛。

current_entries.en_definition: A person from Armenia or of Armenian descent.

examples.english: 'Hayastan' is Armenia's name in Armenian.
examples.chinese: ”哈亚斯坦“是亚美尼亚的名字在亚美尼亚语中。

current_entries.en_definition: A branch of Indo-European languages, which includes the various stages of the Armenian language.
A protective layer over a body, vehicle, or other object intended to deflect or diffuse damaging forces.
Clothing and ornamentation; raiment.
A collection laid out to be viewed in full.
A large collection.

(programming) Any of various data structures designed to hold multiple elements of the same type; especially, a data structure that holds these elements in adjacent memory locations so that they may be retrieved using numeric indices.
(transitive) To seize (someone) with the authority of the law; to take into legal custody. [from 14th c.]
A decorative style of art and architecture that especially used the sinuous and flowing lines of plants.
Clear; effective.

examples.english: Sometimes he has difficulty being articulate about his views.
examples.chinese: 他有时候会难以清楚地表达他的意见。

current_entries.en_definition: (zoology) Any ungulate mammal with an even number of toes and belonging to the Artiodactyla, including pigs, sheep, deer, cattle, and most grazing animals. In contrast, horses have an odd number of toes and as such are not Artiodactyla, but Perissodactyla. [from 19th c.]
(adverbial) Actually, in fact.
(Christianity, specifically) The believed entry of Jesus Christ into heaven after his resurrection.
The fortieth day of Easter, that is the Thursday 39 days after Easter Day.
The largest continent, located east of Europe (typically delimited by the Urals), west of the Pacific Ocean, north of Oceania and south of the Arctic Ocean.

examples.english: Europe has a smaller population than Asia.
examples.chinese: 欧洲的人口比亚洲少。

current_entries.en_definition: (historical) An ancient province of the Roman Empire in western Anatolia, existing between 133 BC (during the Roman Republic) and the 7th century AD.

An incidental remark to a person next to one made discreetly but not in private, audible only to that person. (theatre) A brief comment by a character addressing the audience, unheard by other characters.
(of a look or glance) With disapproval, skepticism, or suspicion.

A sticky, black to brown and highly viscous liquid or semi-solid, composed almost entirely of bitumen with small mineral particles, that is present in most crude petroleums and in some natural deposits.

examples.english: The street is paved with asphalt.
examples.chinese: 這條街鋪上了柏油。

current_entries.en_definition: Ellipsis of asphalt concrete, a hard ground covering used for roads and walkways.
Agreement; act of agreeing.
Something resembling or shaped like an asterisk symbol.
(astronomy) A naturally occurring solid object, which is smaller than a planet and a dwarf planet, larger than a meteoroid and not a comet, that orbits a star and often has an irregular shape.

examples.english: The asteroid broke up into small pieces as it entered Earth's atmosphere.
examples.chinese: 进入地球大气层后，小行星分裂成了无数的小块。

current_entries.en_definition: A steppe in Russia, part of the Eurasian Steppes
(set phrase, idiomatic) Unable to come closer; at a distance.
(modal) After only a superficial examination or review.
Near; soon.
(idiomatic) After a long time; eventually.
(idiomatic) In the end; finally; ultimately.
(focus) At a minimum or lower limit.
For a long time; extensively.
(formal, _, or, _, dated) At last, finally, eventually.
At the most; at a maximum or upper limit.
